name: Pozo de los Humos from Masueco Trail
name_original: Pozo de los Humos – running circular desde Masueco - Parque Natural de las Arribes del Duero
description: Experience the challenging 4.3-mile (6.9 km) Pozo de los Humos circular jogging route from Masueco, featuring a spectacular waterfall.
region_intro: Jogging around Masueco offers routes through the dramatic landscapes of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. The region is characterized by deep canyons and rugged terrain, sculpted by the Duero River. Joggers can explore paths winding through vineyards, olive groves, and almond trees, with granite formations as a prominent feature. The area provides a network of trails suitable for various levels of fitness, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. Best jogging routes around Masueco The most popular jogging route is Pozo de los Humos from Masueco Trail, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 56 minutes to complete. This difficult route leads to the spectacular Pozo de los Humos waterfall. Another top favourite among local runners is Rupurupay Viewpoint Trail, a difficult 4.2 miles (6.7 km) path. This route offers views over the rugged landscape and river canyons. Local runners also love the Pozo de los Humos Trail, a 2.3 miles (3.7 km) trail leading through river valleys and natural scenery, often completed in about 25 minutes. Jogging around Masueco is defined by deep canyons, rugged terrain, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Masueco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 1.0 stars from 1 review. 38 runners have used komoot to explore Masueco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available around Masueco?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;8&lt;/strong&gt; dedicated running trails around Masueco, offering a variety of experiences within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. These routes cater to different fitness levels, from moderate paths to more challenging treks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Masueco?
A: The terrain around Masueco is characterized by its 'abrupta tierra' (abrupt land), featuring deep canyons and rugged landscapes sculpted by the Duero River. You'll find paths winding through vineyards, olive groves, almond trees, and chestnut trees, with prominent granite formations. The routes often involve significant elevation changes, offering a challenging and visually rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes in Masueco for beginners?
A: While most routes around Masueco are considered difficult due to the rugged terrain, there is one moderate route available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993660762/pozo-de-los-humos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 2.3-mile (3.7 km) path that leads through river valleys and natural scenery, making it a good option for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: Which running trails offer the best scenic viewpoints?
A: For breathtaking views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993663461/rupurupay-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rupurupay Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive vistas over the rugged landscape and river canyons. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993657961/pozo-de-los-humos-from-masueco-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos from Masueco Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which culminates at the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993657961/pozo-de-los-humos-from-masueco-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos from Masueco Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that leads directly to the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4230399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. This 50-meter waterfall is especially impressive after heavy rainfall.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Masueco?
A: The running routes in Masueco are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 1.0 stars from 1 review. 38 runners have used komoot to explore Masueco's varied terrain, often praising the dramatic landscapes and the unique experience of running through the Arribes del Duero Natural Park.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Masueco area?
A: Yes, there are circular running options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rupurapay Viewpoint loop from Aldeadávila de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging 8.8 km circular path, allowing you to return to your starting point after enjoying the views.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Masueco?
A: The changing seasons offer varied landscapes for jogging in Masueco. Spring and autumn generally provide milder temperatures and lush scenery, making them ideal. During periods of heavy rainfall, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4230399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is particularly spectacular, creating its namesake 'Well of Smokes'.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. Masueco offers several difficult routes for experienced trail runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rupurapay Viewpoint – Aldeadávila de la Ribera loop from Aldeadávila de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 17.5 km trail with significant elevation changes, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: What kind of natural features can I observe while running in Masueco?
A: While jogging in Masueco, you'll be immersed in the rich biodiversity of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. Keep an eye out for majestic birds like the black stork and the royal eagle, which can be observed along the Duero River. The routes also showcase diverse flora, including vineyards, olive groves, almond trees, and chestnut trees, set against striking granite formations.

Q: Are there any other notable waterfalls besides Pozo de los Humos in the area?
A: Yes, beyond the famous Pozo de los Humos, you can also explore other beautiful waterfalls in the vicinity. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4230380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Pinero&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7214864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faia da Água Alta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are other natural attractions worth discovering, offering additional scenic spots for your runs.
tips: The Pozo de los Humos is an impressive waterfall located in the province of Salamanca, in the autonomous community of Castilla y León, Spain. This waterfall is located on the Uces River and is known for its spectacular waterfall of about 50 meters high, especially impressive during the rainy season. 😊

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this running route?
faqs.answer: This circular running route is approximately 6.9 kilometers long and generally takes about 55-60 minutes to complete for a jogger. Keep in mind that this is a challenging trail, so your actual time may vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pozo de los Humos running trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied and challenging terrain. You'll encounter significant sections of natural paths, unpaved ground, and gravel. There are also parts categorized as 'alpine' terrain and 'Mountain Hiking Path,' indicating potentially rocky and uneven surfaces. Shorter stretches include paved sections and some asphalt. Be prepared for a mix of surfaces that require good footing.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along this route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the spectacular Pozo de los Humos Waterfall, a 50-meter cascade known for its 'smoke' effect. You can experience it from a unique suspended walkway that offers views over the top. The circular route also allows for a visit to the Cascada de Pinero and provides panoramic views of the Uces River and the dramatic granite landscape of the Parque Natural de las Arribes del Duero.

faqs.question: Is this running trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as a 'difficult' run by Komoot and is not recommended for beginner runners. It requires good fitness and sure-footedness due to its varied terrain and significant elevation changes (around 250 meters up and down). While shorter sections near the waterfall might be easier for hiking, the full circular route for jogging demands a higher level of physical preparation.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail from Masueco?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Masueco. While specific parking details aren't provided, Masueco is the common starting point for this circular route. Look for designated parking areas within the village or near the trailhead. Some sections of the gravel track closer to the waterfall may also be accessible by car.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Pozo de los Humos waterfall for running?
faqs.answer: For the most impressive display of the Pozo de los Humos Waterfall and its 'smoke' effect, it's best to visit during late autumn, winter, and spring (December to May). This is when rainfall is more abundant, ensuring a strong water flow. However, be prepared for potentially wet and muddy conditions on the trail during these seasons.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Pozo de los Humos running trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on trails within the Parque Natural de las Arribes del Duero. However, it is crucial to keep them on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to run this trail in the Parque Natural de las Arribes del Duero?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need a specific permit or have to pay an entrance fee to access and run on trails like this within the Parque Natural de las Arribes del Duero. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the natural environment.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural de las Arribes del Duero is known for its diverse wildlife. Along the trail, you might spot various birds of prey soaring above the dramatic landscape. The park is also home to other Mediterranean fauna, so keep an eye out for local species, especially in quieter sections.

faqs.question: Does this running route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular running route from Masueco intersects with sections of other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Etapa 35: Pereña de la Ribera – Masueco, the Ruta del Duero, and various segments of the Mirador Pozo de los Humos trails.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a run on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, it's essential to bring appropriate gear. We recommend sturdy trail running shoes with good grip, sufficient water, and snacks. Depending on the season, consider layers of clothing, sun protection, and a waterproof jacket. A map or GPS device is also advisable, as is a fully charged phone for emergencies.
